texto: Walking through town with Trip, I thought about how easily he had folded me into his group. Sometimes when Didi makes peanut butter cookies, she'll get all cranky trying to blend the peanut butter in the sugar, eggs, and butter. See, the peanut butter always stays in a big clump and the eggs are all slimy and you have to really work at it before everything gets nice and smooth. But the way Trip pulled me into his buttery, sugary life, you'd never know when I was peanut butter in the first place.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Kat Yeh sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Walking through town with Trip, I thought about how easily he had folded me into his group. Somet…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amizade, food, butter, útil em momentos de termino e trabalho.

frase_original: Walking through town with Trip, I thought about how easily he had folded me into his group. Sometimes when Didi makes peanut butter cookies, she'll get all cranky trying to blend the peanut butter in the sugar, eggs, and butter. See, the peanut butter always stays in a big clump and the eggs are all slimy and you have to really work at it before everything gets nice and smooth. But the way Trip pulled me into his buttery, sugary life, you'd never know when I was peanut butter in the first place.

texto: People don't care what you name it, G. They like pie for what's on the inside.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kat Yeh: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “People don't care what you name it, G. They like pie for what's on the inside.” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, words-of-wisdom, people pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
